sourceMovie.title: Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur (2006) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Stand by Me Doraemon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur clicked for you, Stand by Me Doraemon (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Stand by Me Doraemon 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur clicked for you, Stand by Me Doraemon 2 (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur?
faq.answer: Try Stand by Me Doraemon, Stand by Me Doraemon 2, Pokémon the Movie: Volcanion and the Mechanical Marvel—each shares DNA with Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur?
faq.answer: Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur (2006) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Family, Fantasy, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Doraemon Movies" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
